The 3rd umpire is having a look, but Markram seems very confident and Rutherford was happy to go as well, , can't get easier then that and the 3rd umpire does the formality of sending Rutherford back. Lovely bowling from Shamsi - drifted in, dragged Rutherford forward and then spun just enough to catch the outside edge on the push, the ball was dipping low, but Markram leans forward, gets both hands and completes a fine catch. full_text: Alzarri Joseph to Markram, Caught by Mayers!! Needless! Markram tries to take on Joseph first ball and perishes in the process. The partnership was building nicely and the South African skipper has thrown it away. This was in the slot alright but Markram does not get the loft anywhere close to the middle, miscues it straight up and Mayers take a couple of steps back near mid-off before hanging on. West Indies needed that wicket after an expensive previous over.

short_text: Russell to de Kock, Caught by Rutherford!! Caught at deep backward square leg - Russell has two and the crowd has come t
full_text: Russell to de Kock, Caught by Rutherford!! Caught at deep backward square leg - Russell has two and the crowd has come to life at North South. Banged in short and de Kock is rushed for pace, hit high on the bat and he has no control over the pull, lobbed in the air and behind square on the on-side, Rutherford runs across, settles under the skier and takes the catch with his hands pointing down. Now, now - the horrors of the past can come back quickly into South Africa's minds.

full_text: Russell to Reeza Hendricks, Caught by Pooran!! A loud shout for , there was a noise, but was it off the pad? West Indies have , short and angling down leg, Reeza Hendricks was looking to flick and there's a deviation, onto UltraEdge and it's indeed off the bat - a strangle down leg and .
